sourceMovie.title: Breaking News
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Breaking News (2004) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Johnnie To's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Exiled
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Breaking News clicked for you, Exiled (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Hot Fuzz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Breaking News clicked for you, Hot Fuzz (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Breaking News?
faq.answer: Try Exiled, Hot Fuzz, Kiss Kiss Bang Bang—each shares DNA with Breaking News while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Breaking News?
faq.answer: Breaking News (2004) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Breaking News worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
